stem: A product enters the manufacturing pipeline. The probability of passing raw material inspection is 72%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing machining is between 61% and 71% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing assembly is 82%. What is the overall probability that the product is shipped?

ground_truth.answer: P(shipped) ranges from 0.36 to 0.419 as the machining pass rate varies from 61% to 71%. Point estimate: 0.39.

solution_steps: The product must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the machining stage creates a 5.9 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the machining rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
